title.en: Post-ship iteration driven by production data
title.es: Iteración post-lanzamiento impulsada por datos de producción

question.en: Tell me about a project where you shipped, then went back and made it measurably better based on production data. What did the data reveal that pre-ship testing missed?
question.es: Cuéntame sobre un proyecto en el que lanzaste y luego regresaste y lo mejoraste de forma medible basándote en datos de producción. ¿Qué reveló la información que las pruebas previas al lanzamiento no detectaron?

must_include: what was shipped and what the pre-ship assumptions were about its behavior
the specific production signal or metric the candidate monitored — not just 'I watched the dashboards'
the gap between expected behavior and observed production behavior, described with numbers
the improvement the candidate drove based on that production signal
a before-and-after measurement showing the improvement was real and attributable
must_avoid: stories where the post-ship improvement was mandated by an incident or a customer complaint rather than proactive monitoring
conflating shipping a bug fix with iterative improvement — the story should be about raising quality, not fixing broken functionality
claiming the improvement without owning the decision to go back ('the team decided we should iterate')
